perturbation.original_text: The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through CSV or Database format in the NECS Team Developers Zone .
perturbation.changed_text: The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through secure FTP, encrypted email, or physical hard drive. Inria is responsible for delivering the GTL data in a readable format. GTL Data is defined as data delivered via the NECS Team Developers Zone.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mentions CSV or Database format in the NECS Team Developers Zone, whereas, in the modified text, the data can be delivered via secure FTP, encrypted email, or physical hard drive. Then, the last sentence is contradicting itself by stating that GTL Data is defined as data delivered via the NECS Team Developers Zone, so the contradiction is that now, GTL Data can be delivered by multiple ways, creating ambiguity and being contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.GTL DATA. The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through secure FTP, encrypted email, or physical hard drive. Inria is responsible for delivering the GTL data in a readable format. GTL Data is defined as data delivered via the NECS Team Developers Zone. The GTL Data remain the exclusive so le property of Inria. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization. Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria. The obligation of confidentiality will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. Initially, GTL Data can be provided through secure FTP, encrypted email, or physical hard drive in the contradicted text. However, the changed text then defines GTL Data specifically as data delivered via the NECS Team Developers Zone. This creates a clear contradiction because the GTL data is defined in two separate ways, creating ambiguity as to whether the data provided through secure FTP, encrypted email, or physical hard drive is actually considered GTL data, directly fulfilling the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, User will retain a copy of the GTL Data for archival purposes only. However, the user will cease active usage and delete any working copies, and will certify such partial destruction in a letter to Inria. The obligation of confidentiality will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of all GTL Data upon termination, while the changed text allows the User to retain a copy for archival purposes, but says it will delete any working copies. This contradiction will lead to conflicts over compliance with data handling obligations. The contradiction takes place in the paragraph related to GTL Data in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.GTL DATA. The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through CSV or Database format in the NECS Team Developers Zone . The GTL Data remain the exclusive so le property of Inria. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, User will retain a copy of the GTL Data for archival purposes only. However, the user will cease active usage and delete any working copies, and will certify such partial destruction in a letter to Inria. The obligation of confidentiality will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all GTL Data must be returned or destroyed. The changed text allows the User to retain a copy for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the original requirement to return or destroy all GTL Data upon termination, creating an in-text contradiction within Section 5 as it relates to the handling of GTL data after the agreement's termination.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement will only be binding when signed by both Parties in the blanks immediately below. if this document is signed first by Inria, organization will have seven (7) business days from the date of Inria’s signature below in which to return a signed pdf ver sion of this document to Inria via email to the technical contact, or this document will be deemed invalid and of no binding effect .
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement will only be binding when signed by both Parties. If this document is signed first by Inria, organization will have fourteen (14) business days from the date of Inria’s signature below in which to return a signed version of this document to Inria via postal mail to the Adm Contact listed in section 1, or this document will be deemed invalid.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the organization has seven business days to return the signed agreement via email, while the changed text gives them fourteen business days via postal mail. This creates an ambiguity and internal contradiction related to the deadline and method of returning the signed agreement. The original contradiction is made in Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.MISCELLANEOUS . This Agreement will be interpreted in accordance with the laws of the France . Should a dispute arise concerning the interpretation, validity or execution of this agreement, the Parties shall bring the disput e to French Courts, which shall exclusively be competent. This agreement will only be binding when signed by both Parties. If this document is signed first by Inria, organization will have fourteen (14) business days from the date of Inria’s signature below in which to return a signed version of this document to Inria via postal mail to the Adm Contact listed in section 1, or this document will be deemed invalid.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the deadline and method of returning the signed agreement from seven business days via email to fourteen business days via postal mail. The contradicted text, which is essentially the changed text repeated, now directly conflicts with the original text's stipulations. This creates a strong contradiction because the two versions of the clause specify different timelines and delivery methods, rendering the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Inria may terminate this Agreement, or suspend the delivery of data hereunder, for cause at any time if Organization breaches this Agreement or without cause at an y time for whatever reason.
perturbation.changed_text: Inria may terminate this Agreement, or suspend the delivery of data hereunder, for cause only if Organization breaches this Agreement, and Inria provides 30 days written notice. Organization may terminate at any time for any reason with immediate effect.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows Inria to terminate the agreement with or without cause, while the changed text restricts Inria to terminating only for cause with 30 days' notice, but allows the Organization to terminate at any time for any reason, creating a contradiction and asymmetry in termination rights. The in-text contradiction is created within section 3.C 'Duration'
perturbation.contradicted_text: C. Duration . This Agreement will commence on the Effective Date and continue for 365 days thereafter (the “Agreement Term” ), at which point it shall expire unless otherwise extended by Inria, at its sole election. Inria may terminate this Agreement, or suspend the delivery of data hereunder, for cause only if Organization breaches this Agreement, and Inria provides 30 days written notice. Organization may terminate at any time for any reason with immediate effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Inria's termination rights, limiting them to only for-cause terminations with a 30-day notice period, whereas the original text permitted termination with or without cause. This directly contradicts the 'Duration' section, which now also reflects the changed text. The altered termination clause creates a contradiction within the document regarding the circumstances under which Inria can terminate the agreement, as the original clause allowed for termination 'at any time for whatever reason' without notice, a right now removed.

perturbation.original_text: The obligation of confidentiality will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligation of confidentiality will remain in effect for a period of five (5) years following the termination of this agreement, regardless of whether the GTL data is released to public or has fallen into public domain.
perturbation.explanation: The original text links the confidentiality obligation to the public availability of the GTL data, while the changed text establishes a fixed 5-year confidentiality period regardless of public availability. This creates an internal contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations. This affects the section 5 of the contract and the in-text contradiction is made in the GTL Data paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.GTL DATA. The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through CSV or Database format in the NECS Team Developers Zone . The GTL Data remain the exclusive so le property of Inria. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization. Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria. The obligation of confidentiality will remain in effect for a period of five (5) years following the termination of this agreement, regardless of whether the GTL data is released to public or has fallen into public domain.
perturbation.justification: The original text ties confidentiality to the public availability of data, while the changed text sets a fixed 5-year confidentiality period. This creates a contradiction because the original text implies confidentiality ends when data is public, while the changed text enforces it for 5 years regardless. The contradicted text, 5.GTL DATA, reiterates the changed text regarding the 5-year confidentiality period, but this does not remove the fact that there is a contradiction introduced by the Change Text from the original text. Therefore, the change introduces an in-text contradiction as defined in the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.
